stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced Apr 20: Standalone net profit Rs 2,014 Cr (+34.9% YoY from Rs 1,493 Cr); NII Rs 3,702 Cr (+18.8%); FY26 PAT Rs 7,019 Cr (+27.2% YoY); FY26 NII Rs 13,664 Cr (+17.13%). NIM 3.95%; total business Rs 6 lakh Cr (+17.47%); advances +21.74%; deposits +14.14%. Asset quality improved: GNPA 1.45% (from 1.74%); NNPA 0.13% (from 0.18%). Final dividend Rs 1.20/share (12%) + Rs 1 interim already paid = Rs 2.20 total FY26 payout. Board approved sizable FY27 capital raise: Rs 7,500 Cr equity + Rs 10,000 Cr infrastructure bonds. Stock rallied ~5-6.6% intraday Apr 21 post-results to Rs 80.64; up 22.76% over 6 months. Q4 earnings call recording released to investors.
stocks.sector_context: RBI MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 5.25% on Apr 8 (sixth consecutive hold) with neutral stance; FY27 GDP forecast 6.9%, CPI 4.6%. Stable rates protect PSU bank NIMs near term. Nifty PSU Bank index gained 30.5% in CY2025 on improving asset quality and credit growth; PSU banks led rate transmission and continue to outpace private peers on advances growth. Mid-cap PSU banks like BoM benefiting from steady deposit franchise and credit demand. Headwind: stable rates limit fresh re-rating catalyst absent further policy easing.
stocks.peer_comparison: BoM among the highest-profit-growth mid-cap PSU banks: 34.9% Q4 PAT growth materially outpaces SBI (~stable mid-teens) and most large-cap PSU peers. NIM at 3.95% is best-in-class for PSU banks (vs SBI ~3%, PNB ~3%). NNPA 0.13% is among the lowest in PSU bank space. Advances growth 21.74% leads peer set. Capital raise of Rs 7,500 Cr equity is dilutive but signals growth confidence; valuation re-rating room vs SBI/Canara if execution sustains.
red_flags: Rs 7,500 Cr equity raise approved for FY27 - near-term EPS dilution risk and overhang on stock
Stable RBI rate stance limits PSU bank sector re-rating catalyst
Stock already +22.76% in 6 months and +5-6.6% on results day - profit-taking risk
PSU bank advances growth pace may moderate if credit cycle peaks
No ASM/GSM inclusion or SEBI action identified
catalysts: Post Q4 results momentum on 35% PAT growth and record FY26 profit
Rs 1.20/share final dividend post-AGM (record date pending)
FY27 Rs 7,500 Cr equity QIP execution and pricing
Rs 10,000 Cr infra bond issuance
Continued PSU bank credit growth and asset quality improvement

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results Apr 20: Consolidated PAT Rs 655.8 Cr (+19.2% YoY, +26% QoQ); Revenue Rs 2,181.5 Cr; FY26 PAT Rs 2,291 Cr (+18.3%) on total income Rs 8,505 Cr. Retail disbursements all-time high Rs 9,020 Cr (+32%); corporate disbursement re-entry Rs 335 Cr (after ~4 years). AUM/Loan book Rs 90,921 Cr (+13% YoY); Retail loan book Rs 86,946 Cr (+16% YoY). GNPA improved to 0.93% (sub-1% for first time, from 1.08% YoY). Final dividend Rs 8/share recommended. India Ratings UPGRADED NCD/Bank Loan rating from IND AA+ to IND AAA Stable. Stock surged ~11% intraday Apr 21 to Rs 1,006; closed Rs 979 (+8.09%). MOFSL post-result Buy with PT Rs 1,143 (~17% upside). TTM P/E ~10.7-11.0 - large valuation discount to listed HFC peers. Apr 21: Independent Director Nilesh Vikamsey tenure completed.
stocks.sector_context: Indian housing finance/NBFC sector remains structurally positive: NBFC-HFC on-book portfolio +16% YoY in Q1 FY26; sector projected to grow 15-17% in FY26 (HFCs ~18%; affordable housing 22-23%). RBI rate hold at 5.25% supportive of fixed-rate mortgage book. Government affordable-housing push intact. Credit-rating environment strengthening across well-capitalized HFCs - PNBHF AAA upgrade representative. Persistent headwind: bank competition on home-loan pricing keeps yield pressure on HFCs.
stocks.peer_comparison: Trades at clear discount to Bajaj Housing/HDFC peers despite GNPA <1% and 16% retail loan growth - among the cleanest books in HFC space. AAA rating now matches top-tier HFCs, supporting funding cost compression. Disbursement growth +32% YoY retail is sector-leading. Corporate book re-entry differentiates vs pure-retail peers. Post-earnings outperforming HFC peer set; valuation gap closing but still material.
red_flags: Stock already up 8-11% on results day - near-term consolidation/profit-booking risk
Independent Director Nilesh Vikamsey exit Apr 21 - governance optics, though tenure completion is routine
Bank competition on home-loan pricing remains structural margin headwind
Re-rating to peer multiples has been historically slow
Corporate book re-entry brings back episodic credit-cost risk after years of clean retail focus
No ASM/GSM inclusion or SEBI action identified
catalysts: Post-earnings momentum: record FY26 PAT, GNPA <1%, AAA upgrade
Rs 8/share FY26 dividend record date and payout
Funding cost compression flowing from IND AAA upgrade
MOFSL Buy with Rs 1,143 PT - further sell-side upgrades likely
Valuation re-rating as peer-discount narrows

stocks.recent_news: Price Rs 123.42-123.87 on Apr 24, 2026 (intraday). AUM Rs 54,127 Cr (largest gold ETF in India); expense ratio 0.80%. Gold prices on a tear: MCX gold +1.79% intraday early Apr to Rs 1,50,315/10g; touched record highs ~Rs 1,80,779/10g and international ~$5,626/oz during April. Q1 CY2026 saw RECORD quarterly inflows into Indian gold ETFs of INR 316 bn (~$3.45 bn), adding ~20t to holdings; ~80% concentrated in January. March 2026 net inflows Rs 22.7 bn (lowest in 7 months but 11th consecutive monthly inflow). Gold ETF category delivered ~59.6% avg returns since previous Akshaya Tritiya (some up to 61%). Akshaya Tritiya 2026 (Apr 19-20) demand strong; weak rupee at record lows amplifying INR gold gains.
stocks.sector_context: Gold structurally bullish on multiple drivers: (1) Sustained central bank buying; (2) Persistent geopolitical tensions including US tariff overhang; (3) Weakening INR pushing imported gold prices higher; (4) Strong Indian retail/ETF investment demand; (5) Stable RBI rates capping real-yield headwind for gold. Goldman Sachs and domestic experts target Rs 1.7-2.0 lakh/10g over the next 12 months. Near-term outlook from Motilal Oswal: 'buy on dips' with broad range Rs 1.55-1.75 lakh/10g expected post Akshaya Tritiya. Behavioral shift: high physical gold prices pushing investors toward digital/ETF formats - structurally positive for GOLDBEES AUM.
stocks.peer_comparison: GOLDBEES is the LARGEST gold ETF in India by AUM (Rs 54,127 Cr) - liquidity leader vs SBI Gold ETF, HDFC Gold ETF, ICICI Pru Gold ETF, Kotak Gold ETF. Tracks domestic physical gold prices closely. Expense ratio 0.80% is mid-pack (some peers slightly cheaper). Liquidity premium and index-fund-like flows make it the institutional choice for gold beta. Near-term performance mirrors gold price action - up materially with the metal.
red_flags: Gold ETF inflows decelerating: March 2026 was lowest monthly inflow in 7 months - momentum cooling
Akshaya Tritiya 2026 expected to be sentiment booster, not price trigger - demand pulse may already be priced in
Near-term volatility expected per Motilal Oswal - 'buy on dips' implies pullback risk
Technical setup extended after large Q1 rally and record highs
If RBI/Fed surprise hawkish or USD strengthens vs INR, INR gold could correct sharply
ETF tracks gold spot - no idiosyncratic catalyst, purely macro driven
catalysts: Continued central bank gold buying globally
INR weakness amplifying domestic gold price
Geopolitical/tariff escalations driving safe-haven demand
Fed rate cut expectations (lower real yields support gold)
Festival/wedding season demand carrying through to Dhanteras
Continued retail shift from physical to ETF format growing AUM

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results Apr 21 BEAT estimates handily: Net profit Rs 1,110.9 Cr (+27.2% YoY) vs estimate Rs 990-1,010 Cr; Revenue Rs 6,747.79 Cr (+22.6%) vs estimate Rs 6,230-6,370 Cr - record quarterly revenue. EBITDA margin 26.3% holds firm despite 50%+ jump in advertising/promo spend. Double-digit volume-led growth achieved despite broader urban-FMCG slowdown - signals strong brand pull from MAGGI, KITKAT, NESCAFE. Final dividend Rs 5/share for FY26; record date July 10, 2026. Stock surged 6.86% to fresh 52-week high Rs 1,374.70 on Apr 21; trading Rs 1,418-1,425 on Apr 24 (continued upside). Broker upgrades: Citi Buy PT Rs 1,675 (~21.5% upside); Nuvama Buy PT Rs 1,640; Nomura Buy PT Rs 1,500.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty FMCG index down ~6% YTD CY2026 but reclaiming ground - jumped 2.55% on Apr 21 led by Nestle, HUL, Varun Beverages on strong Q4 prints and resilient demand. Urban consumption slowdown remains the dominant sector narrative; rural recovery uneven. Premiumization, beauty/nutrition (10-12% CAGR) and quick-commerce drive selective outperformance. Stable RBI rates supportive of consumer purchasing power. Nestle's volume-led growth in this backdrop is exceptional and validates brand-led pricing power thesis.
stocks.peer_comparison: Clear FMCG OUTPERFORMER in 2026: Q4 PAT growth +27% and revenue +23% materially exceeds HUL (+5.7% revenue Q3) and ITC FMCG (+11% Q3). Margin defense at 26.3% EBITDA despite massive A&P spend differentiates from peers cutting marketing to protect margins. Volume-led growth uniquely strong - sector peers are price-led. Premiumization, distribution depth, e-commerce/QC integration ahead of broader pack. Trades at premium FMCG multiple but earnings momentum justifying valuation.
red_flags: Stock at fresh 52-week high after multiple-day rally - extended technical setup, profit-taking risk
Premium valuation vs FMCG peers - any volume-growth disappointment punished sharply
50%+ A&P spend hike: while revenue-positive, sustainability of margin at 26.3% questioned if input costs spike
Broader urban consumption slowdown still a sector overhang into FY27
Rs 5/share dividend yields ~0.35% - modest payout vs blue-chip FMCG norms
No ASM/GSM inclusion or SEBI action identified
catalysts: Continued post-earnings momentum and broker target upgrades (Citi Rs 1,675, Nuvama Rs 1,640)
Record date July 10 for Rs 5/share final dividend
FY27 volume growth trajectory commentary in upcoming investor interactions
Premiumization/quick-commerce ramp
Possible AGM (August timeline) value announcements

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 audited results and FY26 dividend scheduled for board meeting MONDAY APR 27, 2026 (3 calendar days away); investor/analyst conference call Apr 27 at 6:00 PM IST. Provisional Q4 FY26 business update already disclosed: Total deposits Rs 1,52,660 Cr (+22.8% YoY); Gross advances Rs 1,36,040 Cr (+25.1% YoY) - both indicate strong growth trajectory. Analyst expectations: NIM 5.4-5.6%; Q4 PAT Rs 520-580 Cr. 12-month consensus PT Rs 600-720 (mostly Buy ratings) - but stock already trading well above that range at ~Rs 990-1,007 in Apr 17-20 (52-week high Rs 1,039.20; low Rs 566). P/E 30.19, P/B 3.70. Stock has nearly doubled from 52-wk low.
stocks.sector_context: Small Finance Bank space benefiting from continued credit demand among MSME/retail customers; AU is the largest SFB by balance sheet. RBI rate hold at 5.25% (Apr 8) supports SFB NIMs - SFBs typically run 5%+ NIMs vs PSU banks ~3%. AU's pending universal bank license application is sector-defining catalyst. Broader bank index in steady uptrend post-RBI policy. Headwind: SFB unsecured book quality watch; competition for deposits intensifying.
stocks.peer_comparison: AU is largest SFB by deposits (Rs 1.52 lakh Cr) and advances (Rs 1.36 lakh Cr) - dominant scale vs Equitas, Ujjivan, ESAF. Deposit growth +22.8% YoY and advances +25.1% YoY both materially above SFB peer averages. Trades at premium P/B 3.70 (vs SFB peers 1.5-2.5x) reflecting universal-bank optionality. NIM 5.4-5.6% is at high end of SFB range. Has run from Rs 566 to ~Rs 1,000+ over the year - leadership in SFB outperformance.
red_flags: EVENT RISK: Q4 results Apr 27 - any miss vs Rs 520-580 Cr PAT estimate or weak FY27 guidance could trigger sharp correction
Stock trading WELL ABOVE consensus 12-month PT of Rs 600-720 - valuation stretched, sell-side targets need significant upgrade to justify current Rs 990+
P/E 30.19 and P/B 3.70 are premium even for SFB - leaves little margin of error
Near 52-week high (Rs 1,039) - technical resistance + profit-taking risk into results
Universal banking license decision still pending - delays could disappoint
SFB unsecured/MFI book asset quality remains a sector watch-out
No SEBI/ASM action identified
catalysts: MAJOR: Q4 FY26 results Apr 27 + FY27 guidance + dividend announcement
Apr 27 6PM analyst call - margin and credit-cost commentary
Provisional 25% advances and 23% deposit growth sets bar for strong audited print
Universal banking license progression
Post-result analyst PT upgrades to align with current price
